legal.content.tos_use: By accessing the niwer.dev domain and its associated subdomains (including but not limited to engine.niwer.dev, git.niwer.dev, modrinth.niwer.dev, wakatime.niwer.dev), you agree to use them for lawful purposes only. You are prohibited from attempting to disrupt the website’s security, bypassing authentication measures, or using automated scripts to scrape data.
legal.content.tos_account: To access specific services, including the software framework, you may be required to create an account on engine.niwer.dev. You are entirely responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials (email and password) and for all activities that occur under your account. You agree to notify the administrator immediately of any unauthorized use of your account.
legal.content.tos_limitations: This website and its portfolio are provided "as is" without any warranties. While I strive to keep the information accurate, I cannot guarantee that the site will always be error-free or uninterrupted.
legal.content.tos_applicable_law: These terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Belgium. Any disputes arising from the use of this website shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Belgium.
legal.content.sale_information: The services, products, and user management systems of this shop are primarily operated across the niwer.dev domain and its subdomains.
legal.list.sale_information.2: Registered Office: Located in Belgium (Registered with the Crossroads Bank for Enterprises / BCE). Full physical address is available upon immediate request via the contact form or consultable via the official BCE registry.
legal.content.sale_scope: This shop offers different categories of products and services, each subject to specific delivery and licensing terms:
legal.list.sale_scope.1: Minecraft Mods: Sold as standalone digital products. Delivery is completed instantly via a download link or email after successful payment.
legal.list.sale_scope.2: Steam Games: These products are displayed on this website for informational and promotional purposes only. The actual purchase and delivery are handled exclusively by the third-party platform Steam. I am not responsible for transactions made on Steam.
legal.list.sale_scope.3: Software Framework (Subscription): Access to the framework is provided via a recurring monthly subscription license. Upon successful payment through Stripe, the user is granted access to an external control panel hosted on the subdomain engine.niwer.dev (via my dedicated server) to manage their access.
legal.list.sale_scope.4: Freelance Services: Development and custom services are not sold directly on this shop. Clients must initiate contact via email or the contact form. Every project is subject to a separate, legally binding freelance contract detailing the specific quote, deadlines, and deliverables.
legal.content.sale_prices_payment: All prices displayed on the shop are in Euros (€) and are VAT inclusive. Subscriptions are automatically billed every month until canceled by the user. Payments are securely processed through Stripe. We do not store or have access to your financial information.
legal.content.sale_refund: In accordance with Belgian and EU consumer protection laws, the rules for digital content and services apply as follows:
legal.list.sale_refund.1: Digital Downloads & Licenses: By purchasing a Minecraft mod or subscribing to the framework, you explicitly request immediate access to the digital content and acknowledge that you waive your 14-day right of withdrawal once the download link is provided or access to the engine.niwer.dev panel is granted. All digital sales are final and non-refundable.
legal.list.sale_refund.2: Subscriptions: You can cancel your framework subscription at any time via the user panel. The cancellation will take effect at the end of the current billing cycle. No partial refunds are issued for unused days.
legal.list.sale_refund.3: Freelance Services: Cancellation and refund terms for custom projects are strictly governed by the individual contract signed between the parties before the project begins.
legal.content.sale_delivery: While I strive to maintain 100% uptime for the dedicated server hosting the engine.niwer.dev panel, I cannot be held liable for temporary service interruptions due to maintenance, network failures, or hosting provider issues.
legal.content.sale_applicable_law: These Terms of Sale are governed by the laws of Belgium. In the event of any issue or complaint, please contact me directly at contact@niwer.dev so we can seek an amicable solution. If no resolution can be reached, any legal dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Belgium.
legal.content.data_controller: The data controller for the niwer.dev domain and its subdomains is Erwin Redoté. For any privacy-related questions or to exercise your rights, you can contact me at contact@niwer.dev.
legal.content.data_collection: We only collect personal data that is strictly necessary to run this website and process your orders:
legal.list.data_collection.1: Account Credentials: When you register an account on engine.niwer.dev, we collect and securely process your email address and an encrypted (hashed) version of your password.
legal.list.data_collection.2: Contact and Order Data: Name, email address, and billing address when you make a purchase.
legal.list.data_collection.3: Technical Data: IP address and essential cookies required for basic website functionality and analytics.
legal.content.data_sharing: Your data is used solely to deliver your purchases, process payments, and respond to your messages. Your personal information is never sold, rented, or shared with third parties for marketing purposes.
legal.content.data_rights: Under the GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify, or request the deletion of the personal data we hold (such as your emails). Please note that data stored by third-party payment processors or information required to be kept by Belgian law for legal or accounting purposes cannot be deleted immediately. To exercise your rights, contact me at contact@niwer.dev

content_aboute_me:
Hi, I'm Erwin (aka Niwer).

Self-taught developer with 8+ years of experience. I specialize in Java and web development, building robust applications and exploring new technologies. Always eager for new challenges.

Let's talk!

timeline.first_device: Got my first device, a Playstation 2 from my father
timeline.first_computer: First computer, first youtube video, Of course first Minecraft server giving me a passion for programming !
timeline.secondary_school: Secondary School Begins (General line - economic sciences and management IT)
timeline.end_secondary_school: Obtained CESS (End of secondary school diploma) oriented in management IT and economic sciences
timeline.today: I'm now 21 years old and I'm working as a freelance developer !
store.no_packages_copy: Try another category or come back later.
project.desc.wayz: WayZ is a Minecraft mod made first for a Zombie post-apocalypse server. Secondly the mod has been updated to be community friendly and to be used in any server. It's include a lot of unique features like a custom crafting system, a custom zombie system, a custom 3D Orineted bouding box collisions system, a custom fast loading content-packs system and so much more !
project.desc.wayz_launcher: The WayZ Launcher is a custom launcher for the WayZ server. It simplifies the installation and management of the main mod and the additionnal ones (Sodium, Iris, etc), ensuring that players have the best experience possible. The Launcher is open-source now with some removed features (auto-updater and other network required things).
project.desc.dynamic_smoke_particles: Dynamic Smoke Particles is an actively maintained, open-source Minecraft mod for Fabric and NeoForge (via Modrinth) that introduces realistic, dynamic smoke physics to the game.
project.desc.web_showcase: A website made for my web courses using PHP, HTML and CSS. It include a article/news system, a team system, a department system, a contact form and a much more !
project.desc.lumen: Lumen is a lightweight, extensible, pure Java logging system. Simple console and file logs or custom processors. Available via JitPack. It's perfect for debugging and printing logs in your Java applications.
project.desc.queryon: Queryon is a pure Java SQL builder and parser powered by SQLite. Streamline database interactions with a fluent API and seamless object mapping. Available via JitPack, it's the perfect tool for Java developers seeking efficient and intuitive database management.
project.desc.nebulae: Nebulae is a custom binary file format. It is designed to be simple, efficient, compressed and obfuscated (via a private key system). It can be used to store any kind of data, but it's mainly designed to store game data (maps, entities, etc).
project.desc.integrative_activity: A small game inspired by HEX. Made for my java courses. This include a bridge system, red and blue players and preview system !
project.desc.gaspar: A discord.js bot that include an AI system that can talk with you. It's configurable and can be used in any server.
project.desc.glsl_obfuscator: This is an open-source GLSL obfuscator written in Java. It can be used as a Java library or as a Gradle plugin. It includes many features such as variable renaming, GLSL code minification, and much more.
project.desc.open_vanilla: A Bukkit/Spigot plugin designed to create an enhanced Vanilla Minecraft experience. It includes features such as homes, a hub, administrator commands like /invsee, /ec, /broadcast, a currency system, and much more.
project.desc.bake_n_go: All-in-one order management for bakeries. Streamlines customer orders, inventory tracking, and pickup scheduling via a responsive web-app.
